title: 5 Tests in 10 Seconds
hook: Is that God—or just your thoughts? Test it in 10 seconds.
script: Ask five things: Does this align with Scripture—never against God’s Word? Does it look like Jesus—pure, gentle, willing to yield? Does it bring steady peace, not frantic pressure? Have wise believers confirmed it without flattery? Are doors opening in God’s timing, not forced by fear? If Scripture is a no, it’s not Him. If you have peace, counsel, and providence lining up, take the next small step. Unsure? Pause and pray: “Lord, anchor me in your Word and your will.” Waiting is not wasting; He guides willing hearts.

title: When God Seems Silent: The Five-Fold Check
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: Picture two doors and a swirl of thoughts. Here’s how to discern. First, open your Bible: God never guides against His Word. Second, compare the tone: wisdom from above is pure, peaceable, gentle, and sincere, says James 3:17. Third, check your inner referee—let Christ’s peace rule in your heart, Colossians 3:15. Fourth, invite godly counsel; with many advisers there is safety, Proverbs 11:14. Fifth, notice providence: when He opens, no one shuts, Revelation 3:7. If one fails, wait. If they harmonize, walk humbly and thank Him on the way.

title: Stop Overthinking: A One-Minute Discernment
script: Try this 60-second discernment routine. Breathe. Say, Jesus, speak, your servant is listening. Hold the idea before God. Ask, does Scripture support it? Would Jesus’ character do this—pure, humble, merciful? Do I sense settled peace, not panic? Have mature believers, who tell me the truth, affirmed it? Are doors opening without me forcing them? If Scripture aligns and at least two others agree, take one obedient step today. If not, release it and wait. Close with this prayer: Father, lead me by your Word, your Spirit, your people, and your providence. I choose trust over hurry. Amen.
